description:
GOAL: Add scheduler + spawn/self/send/receive support to the interpreter
paths so the .19 concurrency arc has parity with the JIT path. Discovered
2026-05-11 as a retroactive gap: the .19 arc shipped seven tickets that
work only via the JIT (.11.32 / .11.31 / .19.1-.19.4 / .19.6). User
acknowledged three-path parity as a foundational requirement going
forward; this ticket closes the gap.

## Two interpreter paths

`src/eval.rs::Interp` — AST-level interpreter. Used by `fz test` and
`fz run` CLI today. Has no task/scheduler concept; builtins like
spawn/self/send/receive are entirely absent (eval.rs:51-156 builtin
table).

`src/ir_interp.rs` — IR-level interpreter. Used as a lowering-pipeline
oracle in tests. Currently errors out on Term::Receive and on
BuiltinKind::{Spawn, SelfPid, Send} with "requires the JIT runtime".

Both need parity.

## Deliverables

### eval::Interp

- Introduce a Runtime-equivalent at the Value level: task registry,
  run_queue, mailbox per task, scheduler step.
- Implement spawn, self, send, receive builtins that operate on this
  Value-level runtime.
- Deep-copy at Value level (Values are Rc-shared; deep-copy here is
  about preserving "independent Value graphs per task" — possibly
  just cloning the Rc tree since Values are immutable).
- Wire `fz run` CLI to use the interp runtime so .fz programs using
  spawn work end-to-end through the interpreter.

### ir_interp

- Implement Term::Receive: extract the continuation, look up the
  current task's mailbox in the interp's runtime, fire continuation
  with the message or yield until send arrives.
- Implement BuiltinKind::Spawn / SelfPid / Send.
- Add an InterpRuntime struct paralleling JIT Runtime: tasks map,
  run_queue, scheduler step.

### Shared

- Move the fixture `fixtures/concurrency_ping_pong.fz` to run via the
  interpreter path too (lift the hard-coded-pid assumption if/when
  spawn-with-captures lands, .19.2.x).
- Add a shared test helper `run_fixture_three_ways(fixture_path)` that
  loads the .fz, drives it through eval::Interp, ir_interp, AND JIT,
  and asserts identical outcomes. AOT path stays N/A until .19's AOT
  sub-arc lands.

## Test fixtures

The unit test fixture_ping_pong_via_jit_runtime in src/runtime.rs is
the JIT half. This ticket adds the interp halves.

## Risks

- Scheduler invariants are tricky in the AST interp (Value sharing via
  Rc complicates "independent per-task state"). Mitigation: model
  fresh Process clones; Value's immutability means Rc-shared sub-trees
  are safe to share across tasks (sender's reference doesn't mutate).
- Yield semantics in eval::Interp: the AST interp is recursive, not
  trampolined. Receive's "park and resume" requires either rewriting
  the interp as a state machine OR using a different model (cont
  passing in Rust, async / .await, etc.). Decide at impl time.

## Out of scope

- AOT parity (separate ticket; AOT path itself is dark today).
- Spawn-with-captures (fz-ul4.19.2.x).

## Acceptance

- Same fixtures, same assertions, both interpreter paths green.
- 419 -> ~422 tests; warnings-as-errors clean.
- One commit per sub-path (eval::Interp pass, ir_interp pass).
title: Interpreter parity for concurrency machinery (.19 retroactive)
